records.ety: [L. anxius, fr. angere to cause pain, choke; akin to Gr. 'a`gchein to choke. See Anger.]

defs: Full of anxiety or disquietude; greatly concerned or solicitous, esp. respecting something future or unknown; being in painful suspense; -- applied to persons; as, anxious for the issue of a battle.
Accompanied with, or causing, anxiety; worrying; -- applied to things; as, anxious labor.
quotes: The sweet of life, from which God hath bid dwell far off all anxious cares.

defs: Earnestly desirous; as, anxious to please.
quotes: He sneers alike at those who are anxious to preserve and at those who are eager for reform.
notes: Anxious is followed by for, about, concerning, etc., before the object of solicitude.
